item.title: The moment when shelter dogs find their forever home
item.dc:creator: Grogu007

item.title: To "spite" an "enemy"
item.description: FP Edit: I don't know about y'all but I'm tired. It's been a rough few days. Trying to find some stress relief learning how to fly an F-18 and a digital simulator. Still don't have all the things like a throttle or stick, but we're getting there. The reason I bring this up, it is important for us to distract ourselves with things that we are interested in any chance we can get. Want to help me out? Follow this https://linktr.ee/GridSquares Between the hiding of the Epstein files, and many other inhumane acts. I bring you the newest gross thing that the Trump Administration has done. Read more here https://www.reuters.com/world/us/us-aid-workers-lobbied-weeks-save-food-stocks-destruction-after-trump-cuts-2025-07-16/ Let me know what you think in the comments below this is an outrageous development amongst many
item.dc:creator: JustGridSquares

item.title: ADHD work accommodations to avoid anxiety
item.description: Recently my management team is taking an interest in my work after my upper management complimented me for how organized and detailed my work is, saying i am setting the example of how things should be. But now that means my work is being impacted by people who have no idea what my work is just so they can take credit for it to get the same head pats from upper management. There have been a lot of last minute meetings right before submission that are unnecessary in order to be able to say they worked on something with me but it usually is actually them trying to force something into the project last minute that either makes no sense logistically or is out of scope. Last year after feeling overwhelmed by far too many last minute meetings, I went to HR to request work accommodations for my ADHD to not throw last minute meetings on my calendar as well as including an agenda so I can prepare for it in advance and bring the required information instead of scrambling to find things. They accepted this as it is a reasonable request, as well as having a meeting with my managers to discuss this. So for several months these last minute meetings ended. But now we're almost back to the same level. Today, I stood my ground and refused to join, referencing my work accommodations. They still tried to press me, but I didn't let go. If they're interested in my work, get involved before it's done and submitted and not after!
item.dc:creator: PepperoniAndFingernailPizza

item.title: Freddie Mercury and Annie Lenox
item.description:
at the Ivor Novello Awards in 1987
Queen won the Outstanding Contribution to British Music award. It also was nominated for Best Film Theme or Song for "A Kind of Magic" from Highlander
Eurythmics won Best Contemporary Song for "It's Alright (Baby's Coming Back)". The band also was nominated for Best Song Musically and Lyrically for "The Miracle of Love"
item.dc:creator: Ngugi

item.title: "Look at me, I'm the Captain now."
item.dc:creator: Headcase444

item.title: The artist my heart and soul was always longing for...
item.description:
Fellow imgurians, AURORA - If you don't know her art so far, you should check it out! I know, not everybody likes it. Her music has provocative, experimental and dissonant elements. Her subjects are love, peace, suffering, death and spirituality. One of her main topics is also human connectedness with each other and with nature. There are many dark, sad and aggressive songs. For me, mixed with all the other attributes of her art and her personality (as far as I can judge it as I don't know her personally) she's the perfect artist - the one my heart and soul was always longing for...
I just can't get over it, how extraordinarily good her art is – and by that, I mean her overall performance: That incredibly velvety voice, which can also become immensely powerful. Through her physical expression, you get a much deeper sense of how she perceives the music; she lives the music! She lives the things she sings about! She seems to have an intuitive sense and connection for melody and rythm. Her music and lyrics sound honest, serious, but also loving. And I've never heard this perfectly adapted mix of styles that hit your heart to the point. Also the assembly of her band / background singers seems sophisticatedly aligned with the overall concept. And then there’s her style, which feels truly unique.
For me, it started with the Youtube-Video at Tiny Desk Concert (https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=evBgLWQwAFA&list=RDevBgLWQwAFA&start_radio=1). I was listening to Billie Eilish and Dua Lipa in a time I felt alone, hurt and sad. Then I saw her performance. My first thought was: "Wow, she's weird... Does she mean it?" But she caught me pretty fast, because I realized, that she deeply felt her art - as much as I felt it.
In her interviews and shorts, she tells the weirdest stuff about, e.g., that she might have peed on stage as she was so nervous and that she collects bugs (there's an interview at the end: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=qHPIPdPhUP0&list=RDqHPIPdPhUP0&start_radio=1). In another gig, at the end, she tells the audience, that it's warm up on stage. She smells her armpit and then tells the adience that she smells (https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=P_XguuxLo10&list=RDXXVwFvkNTEg&index=6). Oh yeah, most of the time, she doesn't wear shoes. In the video linked above you can hear her say: "Live is better without shoes!", while she puts off her shoes... In a very beatiful performance in a church (https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=XXVwFvkNTEg&list=RDXXVwFvkNTEg&start_radio=1) she starts to tell a story to the audience about her waking up swaeting, forgets about what she wanted to tell, stops the story and goes on with her performance. Everbody in the audience sympathetically laughs... There's more sympathetically weird stuff... But you'll find out yourself, if you're interested.
You’d expect that someone so young, who found success so early, might be seduced by money, power, public feedback – and of course, I have to repeat that I don’t know her personally, but she seems to come along as very humble, reserved, even shy and at the same time funny and full of humor. Even this morning I exchanged some thoughts with a colleague about her. She said, what she likes most about her IS that she's weird. Moreover, she seems pretty reflected but naïvely reflected at the same time. You realize that when she shares thoughts about her life, emotions, life events, personal development, philosophy and the worlds state. And as soon as you experience her art, you'll feel, that she truly seems to have a message.
I know, probably, I sound to you like a fanatic. But I just feel excited about this artist, as I never experienced sth. similar. And I heard a lot of music throughout my life. AURORA is a truly an exceptional artist – the kind we haven’t seen a very long time. She fuses musical genres, spiritual currents, and a sense of naïve, unconditional love into one beautiful, holistic work of art. I've been listening to almost nothing but her music for months now – and I just can't stop it. It never gets boring or old. For me, she hits my heart, my soul and my mind fastly and deeply, like no artist before. Her music helped me through pretty tough times lately... So, I just want to tell you, check it out, if you didn't before! Maybe you'll enjoy as much I do... It’s also worth mentioning that you can’t just LISTEN to AURORA – you really have to SEE her (e.g. via records of her live performances). And in my opinion, she even sounds better live than on record. I’m so looking forward to my first concert of her – and I hope it’s the first of many, many more! Thanks for reading, you imgurian weirdos! [Front page edit] Holy ..... Didn't expect that! Just wanted to share my favorite music artist... Many thanks, you crazy people!!! But of course, AURORA deserves nothing less ;) Corrected some of my language and content as well... Additionally, I was asked for the song titles / links above, so here you go: #1 Starvation, live on KEXP here: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=26jzm94Ir6Q&list=RD26jzm94Ir6Q&start_radio=1 #2 The Seed, live Haik concert here: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=hRhj3lfWzrI&list=RDhRhj3lfWzrI&start_radio=1 #3 Runaway, live at Tiny Desk here: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=H9e1TDB4c18&list=RDH9e1TDB4c18&start_radio=1 AND Murder Song (5,4,3,2,1), live at 2015 Nobel Peace Prize concert here: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=vh0SWTVwXxw&list=RDvh0SWTVwXxw&start_radio=1 AND Running with the Wolves, live at Tiny Desk here: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=kryVBm5YpCg&list=RDkryVBm5YpCg&start_radio=1 #4 It was the recorded background song Some Type of Skin, live Vevo here: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=hSdieDadgM8&list=RDhSdieDadgM8&start_radio=1 #5 AND #6 Through the Eyes of a Child, live at Nidarosdomen here: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ncG9zTzsisE&list=RDncG9zTzsisE&start_radio=1 Keep being weired! [Edit 2] Germany, half past 2, have to get up in about 3 1/2 h... I didn't expect front page tonight... And that is not what I enjoyed most... It was really lovely, refreshing, interesting and exciting to chat and share with you'll, my lovely imgurians... Many thanks to you for this nice evening, even if I'll be standing tired in front of my students tomorrow, trying to tell them sth about statistics... Let's see, wish me luck and till next time! Good night! [Edit 3] Corrected and completed the link list above.
item.dc:creator: mythm2707

item.title: Memes/tomfoolery
item.description: Huge ass tomato. I'd be on that thing like the very hungry caterpillar. I'd fly that thing to the empire state building with my large bug friends. I'd purposely slip on a banana peel just so I could fall face first into this thing. AND HEIRLOOM?! I'd do whatever the opposite of a hate crime is to that thing!

16 So adorable. One of my favorite videos.
17
18
19
20
21
22
23
24
25
Man Tax: Johnny Tri Nguyen “When you get to a fork in the road, take it.” - Yogi Berra I try to keep my posts "good vibe zones". So, instead of finding the one thing in here you don't like and commenting on it, maybe let it go, don't comment on it and find something that you do like. Love you long time. So, I made a discord server so you guys can donate memes to the museum for future CCC posts. I don't want to run out! Also, we can hang out, play some trivia, talk about movies & tv and, apparently, plucked chicken buttholes. https://discord.gg/AWFV7FT3RQ
item.dc:creator: MemeMuseumCurator

item.title: I have too many memes: Dump #147
item.dc:creator: Sweenerd

item.title: The world: “Are you high, or just incredibly stupid?”
item.description: https://bsky.app/profile/economist.com/post/3lu3ryxzu4726 This is a rare one for me, i don’t usually post without reading the article, even if I don’t post the full screenshots. But I am not giving that website any traffic or clicks, whatever makes them money. This is an advanced stupidity, it’s just as simple as that. There are no layers here, it seems that the head of America’s energy policy is really that evil or stupid and is trying to downplay the effects of climate change as a “by product of a better life” (context clues gathered from feedback on the article). Never mind that the editor at the economist needs a high five, in the face, with a chair for approving this propaganda hit piece, responsible journalism is no longer a concept in America (But can Biden do 50 pushups and find Hillary’s hidden email server? The people have a right to know- cnn or some shit) President Discharge hates windmills, so now the entire country has to suffer through regressive energy policies that will only benefit fossil fuel industry. This article gives shitty people a reason to stop doing the bare minimum we were doing to help stop or slow down climate change. Shame on the economist, and the jackass who wrote the piece, y’all are an embarrassment. Note: Yes, the title is stolen from South Park, it’s the ladder to heaven episode and someone at the UN (or Congress) asks that question to GWB when he tells them that Sadam Hussein has WMD in heaven so they have to bomb heaven. Not sure why I just wrote all this but I’m not deleting it, it won’t be a rant. End rant
item.dc:creator: DownvotesStarWars

item.title: Dobranoc!
item.description:
Sometimes Frank wants the uppies.
Just so he can be Frank and take a nap. No video games for you. Just hold the Frank.
Look at him.
Then he wakes up. And the craziness begins.
We tried the harness today. Yeah. Tried.
Here he is, tactical rolling his way out of the harness.
Bonus Chessie tax, because she wanted to be held while she slept too. Time to clean up a bit and get to sleep. Miłego wieczoru, friends. Hope your evening is peaceful and full of love.
item.dc:creator: FrankTheKitten

item.title: Cześć! Jak się masz?’
item.description:
Frank is doing well. Today was a good day
Some play time
Some more play time
Looking at all the possibilities
And naps
Lots of naps
The fish remains Frank's current nemesis. It's a gorgeous day out. I think I'd like to win the lottery just so I can stay home with Frank every day. Anyone have the numbers for this week's Powerball?
item.dc:creator: FrankTheKitten
